mission.title: kong: Unable to set datadog hostname or port with environment variables
mission.description: Unable to set datadog hostname or port with environment variables. This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify kong troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kong deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kong configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kong configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl get configmap -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kong
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### Kong version (`$ kong version`)

2.6

### Current Behavior

When I try to use `KONG_DATADOG_AGENT_HOST`, the Datadog plugin uses the default `localhost`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Unable to set datadog hostname or port with environmentâ€¦
steps.description:
The workaround we used:

* Update to Kubernetes 1.21
* Set the `ServiceInternalTrafficPolicy` feature gate to `true`
* In your Datadog helm chart, set `agents.localService.forceLocalServiceEnabled=true`

Or, if you upgrade to k8s 1.22, you can skip the other steps; the
```yaml
apiVersion: configuration.konghq.com/v1
 kind: KongClusterPlugin
 metadata:
  name: datadog
  annotations:
     kubernetes.io/ingress.class: kong
   labels:
     global: "true"
 config:
   host: 
   port: 8125
   metrics:
   prefix: api-gateway
```

steps.title: Upgrade kong to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kong:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kong kong/kong --namespace kong
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong
helm list -n kong
```

steps.title: Confirm Unable to set datadog hostname or port withâ€¦ is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kong --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The workaround we used:

* Update to Kubernetes 1.21
* Set the `ServiceInternalTrafficPolicy` feature gate to `true`
* In your Datadog helm chart, set `agents.localService.forceLocalServiceEnabled=true`

Or, if you upgrade to k8s 1.22, you can skip the other steps; the `ServiceInternalTrafficPolicy` gate defaults to True, as does the DD helm chart flag.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kong installed or the issue environment reproducible.
